source_jobs.title: Financial Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

A profitable, family-run, growing group of companies in the Industrial Packaging and Distribution space. Proudly Canadian and entrepreneurial, with multiple divisions.

We serve customers across North America, with core operations and leadership based in Canada. With 60+ employees and a tightly run finance team, we’re scaling while maintaining a practical, no-politics, get-it-done culture.




HOW WE WORK

Finance is here to drive decisions, not just report numbers.

This role reports to the VP Finance & Controller. You'll work across all our divisions, owning financial analysis, reporting, and business insights. You’ll partner closely with leadership to understand performance, identify gaps, and bring clarity to complex financial data.

You’ll thrive here if you enjoy digging into the numbers, asking “why,” and influencing decisions through data, not just building reports.




ABOUT YOU

You’re a strong Financial Analyst who understands how a business actually runs, not just how to build spreadsheets.

You’re comfortable working with large datasets, high transaction volumes, and imperfect data. You don’t wait for direction; you proactively investigate variances, identify trends, and surface insights that matter.

You’re looking for a role where you can have real visibility and impact across a business, not just sit in a silo.




YOU BRING:

 - Strong experience with budget vs. actuals, variance analysis, and financial modeling

 - Ability to translate financial data into clear, actionable business insights

 - Experience working in high-volume, product-based or distribution environments

 - Ownership of monthly reporting packages and performance analysis

 - Comfort working across multiple divisions or business units

 - Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, modeling)

 - Ability to work directly with leadership and non-finance stakeholders


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 3+ years of experience in Financial Analysis / FP&A (Intermediate to Senior level)

 - Strong understanding of financial statements and business drivers

 - Experience with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis

 - Experience working with ERP systems (ex. Epicor Prophet 21 is a strong asset)

 - Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities across teams

 - CPA (or in progress) is preferred but not required


BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience in industrial distribution or product-based businesses

 - Exposure to inventory and COGS-heavy environments

 - Experience working in a multi-entity structure


PERKS & DETAILS:

 - Full-time, on-site (5 days/week) with flexible hours

 - Location: Etobicoke / Airport area (427/401)

 - Competitive salary ($75-100k base) + bonus + benefits

 - High visibility role with direct exposure to leadership

source_jobs.title: Accounting Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

A profitable, family-run, growing group of companies in the Industrial Packaging and Distribution space. Proudly Canadian and entrepreneurial, with multiple divisions.

We serve customers across North America, with core operations and leadership based in Canada. With 60+ employees and a tightly run finance team, we’re scaling while maintaining a practical, no-politics, get-it-done culture.




HOW WE WORK

We run a lean, high-output finance team where execution matters.

This role supports day-to-day accounting operations across multiple divisions, working closely with the Controller and Finance team. You’ll play a key role in ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and structure in a high-volume environment.

This is a hands-on role, ideal for someone who takes pride in clean books, strong processes, and getting things right the first time.




ABOUT YOU

You’re a detail-oriented Accountant who takes ownership of your work and doesn’t cut corners.

You’re comfortable operating in a fast-paced environment with high transaction volume. You understand how accounting flows through the business and can manage multiple responsibilities without dropping the ball.

You don’t need constant direction; you know what needs to get done, and you do it.




YOU BRING:

 - Strong experience with GL accounting, reconciliations, and journal entries

 - Support for month-end close and financial reporting

 - Experience working with high-volume transactions (AP/AR, invoicing, payments)

 - Ability to maintain accuracy and organization under pressure

 - Familiarity with ERP systems (Epicor Prophet 21 or similar preferred)

 - Strong Excel skills and attention to detail




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 2+ years of general accounting experience (AP/AR) at a professional level in a corporate environment

 - Strong understanding of accounting fundamentals (GAAP)

 - Experience supporting the month-end close

 - Experience in a product-based or distribution environment is preferred

 - Highly organized with strong attention to detail

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines


BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience with inventory accounting

 - Exposure to multi-entity environments

 - Working toward CPA designation


PERKS & DETAILS:

 - Full-time, on-site (5 days/week) with flexible hours

 - Location: Etobicoke / Airport area (427/401)

 - Competitive salary + benefits

 - Stable company with long-term growth

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

A profitable, family-run, growing group of companies in the Industrial Packaging and Distribution space. Proudly Canadian and entrepreneurial, with multiple divisions.

We serve customers across North America, with core operations and leadership based in Canada. With 60+ employees and a tightly run finance team, we’re scaling while maintaining a practical, no-politics, get-it-done culture.




HOW WE WORK

Data should drive decisions, not sit in dashboards.

This role works across Finance, Sales, and Marketing to provide visibility into performance and help guide decision-making.

You’ll own reporting and analytics across the business, building dashboards, working with large datasets, and partnering with stakeholders to understand what’s driving results.

This is a hands-on role with a strong execution focus, but you’ll also be expected to go beyond reporting and help teams understand what the data means.




ABOUT YOU

You’re a detail-oriented Data Analyst who enjoys working with both data and people.

You’re comfortable building dashboards, working with large datasets, and ensuring data accuracy. You don’t just deliver reports, you take the time to understand the business context and explain insights clearly.

You’re reliable, organized, and proactive in identifying opportunities to improve reporting and data processes.




YOU BRING:

 - Strong experience building and maintaining Power BI dashboards (DAX, Power Query preferred)

 - Ability to extract, structure, and analyze large datasets (Snowflake or similar)

 - Experience ensuring data accuracy through validation, cleansing, and quality checks

 - Strong SQL skills for data extraction and transformation

 - Ability to translate data into clear, practical insights for business teams

 - Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders (Finance, Sales, or Marketing)

 - Advanced Excel skills for data manipulation and analysis

 - Strong attention to detail and ability to stay organized in a high-volume environment




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 3–5+ years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or similar roles

 - Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Analytics, or related field

 - Strong understanding of business metrics (revenue, sales performance, margins, etc.)

 - Experience working with ERP systems or internal business tools

 - Strong communication skills with non-technical stakeholders

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment




BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience with Snowflake, PostgreSQL, or Azure-based environments

 - Experience with data pipeline tools (e.g., FiveTran)

 - Exposure to Power Automate or workflow automation tools

 - Experience in distribution, logistics, or product-based businesses

 - Exposure to pricing, margin, or sales analytics




PERKS & DETAILS:

 - Full-time, on-site (5 days/week) with flexible hours

 - Location: Etobicoke / Airport area

 - Compensation: $65,000 – $80,000 base + benefits

 - Cross-functional exposure across Finance, Sales, and Marketing

 - Stable company with long-term growth potential

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer (React, Python)
source_jobs.description:
A Few Notes:

 - Profitable B2B SaaS company, teams are based out of North America

 - Role is 95% remote in Toronto (we meetup 1x a month).

 - Must be able to legally work in Canada (visa or sponsorship won't be provided)

 - Our Eng team is growing and we are looking to hire a Full Stack Python/React Developer for an applications team

 - We're open on seniority from Upper Intermediate to Staff level

 - This is a full-stack developer role (but we're open to folks that lean more on the back-end)



About Us:

We're one of the top retail analytic platforms that help marketing teams/brands understand their retail data and run targeted media campaigns without writing code. We help our clients better understand their customers and improve their ROI on campaigns. One of our main customers is Home Depot.
Tech Stack: Python, Django, React, Typescript, Azure.



How We Work:

 - Collaborative team! Lots of new interesting complex work is available

 - We encourage exploration of new technologies - we're a modern tech company

 - We build for the long term, already profitable and focused on quality

 - 95% remote, we meet 1X a month in Toronto and 2X a year at a company retreat



About You:

We are looking for an outstanding Senior/Staff/Lead Full Stack Engineer who wants to play a key role on our applications team.



Example Responsibilities:

 - Working on the whole stack: End-to-end client feature development (from design to delivery)

 - Complex Integrations work: Working with heavy hitters like Meta, Tik Tok, Snapchat on the ad tech side.

 - Dashboards: Improving the UI experience for our Enterprise Clients



Qualifications:

 - 6+ years of full stack experience building enterprise-level B2B/B2C SaaS Applications

 - Strong knowledge of Javascript/Typescript/React and Python

 - Note: We're open to non Python Devs if they have great experience and are willing to make the switch over

 - Experience owning an application end-to-end (ex. zero to-one on a new feature)

 - Strong understanding of scalable system design for web-based APIs

 - Previous ("startup") experience working on a small team with high ownership

 - Experience building and architecting distributed systems (deploying over a public cloud)

 - Write test suites (we aim for 85% or higher test coverage) and deploy using our CI/CD pipeline

 - Nice to have: Adtech or Martech experience





Target FT Salary Range:

 - $130,000 - $180,000* base CDN a year, with Equity & Health Benefits

 - *Comp range be higher for Staff level Devs



Benefits:

Meritocracy 💰

- Leadership opportunities as we scale

- Equity/Options grants

Generous Time Off  🏝

- Flexible remote work policies

Platinum Benefits 🧑🏻

- Solid Health Insurance Plan on Day 1

Learn and Grow 💻

- Coaching/mentoring for your professional development

source_jobs.title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
source_jobs.description:
A Few Notes:

 - Profitable B2B SaaS company, teams are based out of North America

 - Role is 95% remote in Toronto (we meetup 1x a month).

 - Must be able to legally work in Canada (visa or sponsorship won't be provided)

 - Our Platform is growing and we are looking to hire a Senior Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) / Cloud Engineer

 - Our main Cloud Platform is Azure (those with Azure will be prioritized first)



About Us:

We're one of the top retail analytic platforms that help marketing teams/brands understand their retail data and run targeted media campaigns without writing code. We help our clients better understand their customers and improve their ROI on campaigns. One of our main customers is Home Depot.

 - Modern Cloud Stack: Azure is our primary cloud. CI/CD, Containerization, Distributed computing.



About You:

We are looking for an outstanding Senior SRE/Cloud Engineer who wants to play a key role in running our Cloud Ops (ensuring uptime, reliability, CI/CD, Containerization, and automation)



Example Responsibilities:

 - Collaborate with software engineering teams to design, implement, and maintain CI/CD pipelines, enabling rapid and reliable software releases.

 - Automate and optimize our infrastructure provisioning, configuration, and management processes using industry-standard tools and best practices.

 - Implement and manage containerization and orchestration technologies to enhance scalability and resource utilization.

 - Take ownership of the end-to-end availability and performance of our cloud infrastructure; proactively identifying potential issues, and implementing automation to prevent the recurrence of problems.

 - Participate in an on-call rotation, ensuring our systems remain stable and responsive even during off-hours.

 - Lead the development, implementation, and achievement of service-level objectives that are instrumental in maintaining product reliability.

 - Maintain and enhance version control systems and repositories for codebase management.

 - Steer and drive the SRE / DevOps roadmap, assuming full ownership while actively engaging in negotiation and strategic planning to ensure its successful execution.

 - Stay current with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in SRE, DevOps, and automation.



Qualifications:

 - 5 years plus of experience as a Site Reliability Engineer or DevOps Engineer, working with software and infrastructure.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Experience in one or more of the following: Python, Javascript, Ruby, Groovy, PHP, or Bash.

 - Experience in one of the cloud platforms: Azure, AWS, or GCP. (Azure is our main Cloud Platform)

 - Led and built cloud infrastructure projects (zero to one, automation, start up experience)

 - Nice to have: Experience with high availability systems.

 - Nice to have: Experience troubleshooting and debugging production code.

 - Nice to have: Experience with application deployment and data pipelines.

 - Nice to have: Understanding of distributed computing systems.

 - Nice to have: Experience with Snowflake and/or relational databases



Target FT Salary Range:

 - $130,000 - $180,000* base CDN a year, with Equity & Health Benefits

 - *Comp range is higher for Staff level



Benefits:

Meritocracy 💰

- Leadership opportunities as we scale

- Equity/Options grants

Generous Time Off  🏝

- Flexible remote work policies

Platinum Benefits 🧑🏻

- Solid Health Insurance Plan on Day 1

Learn and Grow 💻

- Coaching/mentoring for your professional development

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Python Developer
source_jobs.description:
Senior/Staff Python Developer

 - Role: Full-time, hybrid role based in Downtown Toronto

 - Level: Senior to Staff (IC) seniority

 - Compensation: $150,000 to $200,000 base CAD (depending on Seniority)

 - Focus: This is a Back-end focused Product Role




HOW WE WORK

We don’t separate backend and frontend. Engineers here own problems end to end.

This role is primarily backend focused using Python and Flask, but you will also work on server-side rendered frontend using HTMX and GraphQL APIs.

You’ll spend a significant portion of your time building and shipping features, debugging customer issues, and working on integrations across a complex system.

You’ll regularly switch between building new features and resolving real customer issues, often with evolving requirements. This is a high-ownership environment where priorities can shift quickly, requiring strong problem-solving, adaptability, and decisive execution.




ABOUT YOU

You are a product-minded engineer who focuses on solving problems, not just writing code.

You are comfortable working in ambiguity and take ownership of figuring things out. Whether it is unclear requirements, customer pain points, or a complex codebase, you are able to break things down and move forward.

You ask the right questions, think in systems, and care about outcomes over output.




YOU BRING:

 - Strong experience building backend systems in Python (Flask, Django, or similar)

 - Experience working with microservices-based architectures

 - Ability to own features end to end from design through production and support

 - Experience working with GraphQL or REST APIs

 - Strong debugging skills and ability to handle customer-facing production issues

 - Experience integrating with third-party systems

 - Comfort working across the stack in a backend-heavy environment

 - Ability to work through ambiguity and clarify direction before execution




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 5 to 8+ years of software engineering experience at an enterprise level

 - Strong backend fundamentals and system design experience

 - Experience working in high-ownership, fast-paced environments

 - Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

 - Comfortable managing multiple priorities and switching context




BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience with server-side rendered frontend (HTMX, Turbo, or similar)

 - Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP)

 - Experience in fintech, accounting systems, logistics, or operational software

 - Experience working on complex, domain-heavy products

 - Experience mentoring or supporting other engineers




PERKS & DETAILS:

 - Compensation: $150,000 to $200,000 base CAD (depending on Seniority)

 - High ownership role with direct impact on product and customers

 - Opportunity to work on a complex system with real-world operational impact

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer (Python)
source_jobs.description:
A Few Notes:

 - Profitable B2B SaaS company, teams are based out of North America

 - Role is 95% remote in Toronto (we meet up 1x a month) - we're going to focus on GTA candidates first

 - Must be able to legally work in Canada (visa or sponsorship won't be provided)

 - We're growing, and we are looking to hire a BE Python Developer for the Platform team (We have a separate Applications)

 - Role = 80% BE Platform + 20% Infrastructure/DevOps

 - Open on seniority from Senior to Staff level



About Us:

We're one of the top retail analytics platforms that help marketing teams/brands understand their retail data and run targeted media campaigns without writing code. We help our clients better understand their customers and improve their ROI on campaigns. One of our main customers is Home Depot.
Tech Stack: Python, Django, Celery, React, Typescript, Azure.



How We Work:

 - Collaborative team! Lots of interesting/complex work is available

 - This is a builder role - we're looking for folks who enjoy working in a fast paced company

 - We encourage exploration of new technologies - we're a modern tech company

 - We build for the long term and are already profitable/focused on quality

 - 95% remote, we meetup 1X a month in Toronto and 2X a year at a company retreat



About You:

We are looking for an outstanding Senior/Staff/Lead Platform Engineer who wants to play a core role in building for our platform team. This role will be 100% back-end focused (no FE work)



Example Responsibilities:

- New API Design: Working on making the platform more scalable and data performant.

- Complex Integrations work: Working with heavy hitters like Meta, Tik Tok, Snapchat on the ad tech side.

- Lead Async task management: We're using Celery, we'd like you to take this over and optimize how we do concurrency, distributed tasks, resource intensive jobs.



Qualifications:

 - 6+ years of experience building and scaling services in Python

 - Solid understanding of datastores (ex. PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis) and messaging systems (ex. Kafka, RabbitMQ, SQS, Pub/Sub)

 - Proven experience designing and maintaining high-availability, distributed backend systems (event-driven architectures, background workers, schedulers, queues - we use Azure)

 - Design, implementation, and maintenance of API Services

 - Has led complex feature development for an enterprise level B2B/B2C Saas Platform

 - Async optimization experience (we use Celery)

 - Write test suites and deploy using our CI/CD pipeline

 - Experience owning an application end-to-end, and can understand/address infrastructure concerns

 - Clear communicator who can document systems, explain trade-offs, and influence technical direction



Target FT Salary Range:

 - $130,000 - $180,000* base CDN a year, with Equity & Health Benefits

 - *Comp range can be higher for Staff level Devs



Benefits:

Meritocracy 💰

- Leadership opportunities as we scale

- Equity/Options grants

Generous Time Off  🏝

- Flexible remote work policies

Platinum Benefits 🧑🏻

- Solid Health Insurance Plan

Learn and Grow 💻

- Coaching/mentoring for your professional development

notes: B2B SaaS company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, mostly remote work opportunities
